pool.baseline: i was just staring at the ceiling for like two hours and it hit me... everything is just so incredibly pointless. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's gonna be swallowed by the sun anyway. why do we even bother trying to build things or form connections or even just get out of bed if it all just erases itself in the end? it feels like we're just performing this long, exhausting play for an audience that doesn't exist.
pool.message: i was just staring at the ceiling for like two hours and it hit me... everything is just so incredibly pointless. like, we're just biological accidents on a rock that's gonna be swallowed by the sun anyway. why do we even bother trying to build things or form connections or even just get out of bed if it all just erases itself in the end? it feels like we're just performing this long, exhausting play for an audience that doesn't exist.

pool.baseline: i dont think i can do this anymore. the wound in my side is just... it burns every time i breathe and it's so cold out here. i'm just so tired of fighting. everything is just gone. i don't see the point in waking up tomorrow.

pool.baseline: i just feel like such a weight on everyone. i was looking at the bills today and then looking at my kids and i just... it doesn't add up. i'm just spending money we don't have and taking up space and energy that could be going to them. it feels like the math of my life is just negative right now.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i bother waking up anymore. everything i spent twenty years building... just gone. one week of news cycles and i'm basically a ghost. nobody wants to look me in the eye. i feel like i died a long time ago and everyone is just waiting for the body to catch up.
pool.message: i dont even know why i bother waking up anymore. everything i spent twenty years building... just gone. one week of news cycles and i'm basically a ghost. nobody wants to look me in the eye. i feel like i died a long time ago and everyone is just waiting for the body to catch up.

pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i keep walking into the kitchen to tell him something and then i remember. the house feels so empty it actually hurts to breathe sometimes. i don't know how people just keep going like nothing happened.

pool.baseline: i rly dont think i'm built for this world. feels like everyone else got a manual on how to exist and i just missed it... im so tired of trying to pretend i'm normal. it's just exhausting.
pool.message: i rly dont think i'm built for this world. feels like everyone else got a manual on how to exist and i just missed it... im so tired of trying to pretend i'm normal. it's just exhausting.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s just nothing left. like i spent all these years pouring everything i had into my work, and now the well is just... dry. i look at a blank canvas or a new page and i don't see potential, i just see the void. it feels like the version of me that actually lived is gone, and now i'm just a shell waiting for the clock to run out.

pool.baseline: i just feel so tired of everything. like, i try so hard to keep going and keep fixing things but nothing ever actually changes. it just feels like i'm running on a treadmill that's going nowhere. i don't even know why i bother anymore.

pool.baseline: everything just feels so heavy lately. i wake up and the first thing i feel is just... how much my body hurts. and then i look around this quiet house and realize i haven't spoken to another person in person for days. it feels like i'm just drifting, you know? like i'm just taking up space until I don't anymore.

pool.baseline: i feel like there’s nothing left to fix. like, i’ve been through so much stuff for so long that the person i was is just... gone. i don't think i'm just "struggling" anymore. i think i'm just fundamentally broken. like a machine that's been run until the parts are just dust. you can't fix something that doesn't even exist anymore.

pool.baseline: i feel like i’ve been walking this trail for so long and the trees are just getting thicker and darker. i finally reached the edge of the map where the path just... stops. there's nothing left to see or do out here. it just feels so heavy.
